testcase.name: Joist::Events::Middleware::MemoryIdempotencyStorage#mark_processed marks message as processed with ttl
Joist::Events::Middleware::MemoryIdempotencyStorage#mark_processed updates expiry for already processed message
Joist::Events::Middleware::MemoryIdempotencyStorage#processed? returns true for processed message within ttl
Joist::Events::Middleware::Chain#empty? returns true when no middlewares added
Joist::Events::Middleware::Chain#empty? returns false when middlewares present
Joist::Events::Middleware::Chain#add raises error if middleware doesn't respond to call
Joist::Events::Middleware::Chain#add adds middleware to the chain
Joist::Events::Middleware::Chain#call stops execution if middleware doesn't call block
Joist::Events::Middleware::Chain#call executes final block when chain is empty
Joist::Events::Middleware::Chain#call passes message to each middleware
Joist::Events::Middleware::Chain#call allows middleware to modify result
Joist::Events::Middleware::Chain#call returns result from final block
Joist::Events::Subscriber.new uses default adapter if not specified
Joist::Events::Subscriber.new creates subscriber with default options
Joist::Events::Subscriber.new raises error if subscriber_name not set
Joist::Events::Subscriber.new raises error if topic not set
Joist::Events::Subscriber.new when adapter is configured creates subscriber with adapter
Joist::Events::Subscriber class methods allows setting subscriber_name
Joist::Events::Subscriber class methods allows setting topic
Joist::Events::Subscriber#process_message (private) raises SubscribeError for unknown format
Joist::Events::Subscriber#process_message (private) parses JSON string to Message
Joist::Events::Subscriber#consume! raises NotImplementedError if not overridden
Joist::Events::Message#hash allows messages to be used as hash keys
Joist::Events::Message#hash returns same hash for equal messages
Joist::Events::Message.from_h creates message from hash with symbol keys
Joist::Events::Message.from_h creates message from hash with string keys
Joist::Events::Message.from_h parses timestamp from ISO8601 string
Joist::Events::Message.from_json creates message from JSON string
Joist::Events::Message.new creates a message with required fields
Joist::Events::Message.new auto-generates UUID if id not provided
Joist::Events::Message.new raises error if topic is nil
Joist::Events::Message.new defaults timestamp to current time
Joist::Events::Message.new raises error if payload is not a Hash
Joist::Events::Message.new raises error if topic is empty
Joist::Events::Message.new raises error if timestamp is not a Time object
Joist::Events::Message#== returns true for messages with same id, topic, and payload
Joist::Events::Message#== returns false when comparing with non-Message object
Joist::Events::Message#== returns false for messages with different topic
Joist::Events::Message#== returns false for messages with different payload
Joist::Events::Message#== returns false for messages with different id
Joist::Events::Middleware::Idempotency#call uses configured ttl when marking processed
Joist::Events::Middleware::Idempotency#call doesn't mark as processed if block raises error
Joist::Events::Middleware::Idempotency#call marks message as processed after successful execution
Joist::Events::Middleware::Idempotency.new creates middleware with storage and ttl
Joist::Events::Middleware::Idempotency.new raises error if storage doesn't implement required methods
Joist::Events::Adapters::GcpAdapter#subscribe logs errors but doesn't crash on processing failure
Joist::Events::Adapters::GcpAdapter#subscribe passes options through to GCP listener
Joist::Events::Adapters::GcpAdapter#healthy? returns false if pubsub raises error
Joist::Events::Adapters::GcpAdapter#healthy? returns true if pubsub client is healthy
Joist::Events::Adapters::GcpAdapter#stop stops the subscriber if present
Joist::Events::Adapters::GcpAdapter#stop does not raise if no subscriber
Joist::Events::Adapters::GcpAdapter#publish raises error if topic not found
Joist::Events::Adapters::GcpAdapter#publish publishes message to topic and returns message ID
Joist::Events::Middleware::MemoryMetricsBackend#timing records timing with name and duration
Joist::Events::Middleware::MemoryMetricsBackend#increment tracks counters with different tags separately
Joist::Events::Configuration#validate! returns true when configuration is valid
Joist::Events::Configuration#validate! raises error when no adapters configured
Joist::Events::Configuration#validate! raises error when default adapter not in configured adapters
Joist::Events::Configuration#enable_dual_write returns empty array when dual-write not enabled
Joist::Events::Configuration#enable_dual_write enables dual-write mode for multiple adapters
Joist::Events::Adapters::MemoryAdapter running? returns true after start
Joist::Events::Adapters::MemoryAdapter running? returns false after stop
Joist::Events::Adapters::MemoryAdapter#publish returns message ID by default (wait_for_ack: true)
Joist::Events::Adapters::MemoryAdapter#subscribe only receives messages for subscribed topic
Joist::Events::Adapters::MemoryAdapter#subscriber_count returns count of subscribers for topic
Joist::Events::Adapters::MemoryAdapter#messages_for returns empty array for unknown topic
Joist::Events::Adapters::AmazonMqAdapter#publish publishes message to exchange and returns message ID
Joist::Events::Adapters::AmazonMqAdapter#subscribe nacks and requeues message on error with manual ack
Joist::Events::Adapters::AmazonMqAdapter#subscribe binds queue to exchange with routing key
Joist::Events::Adapters::AmazonMqAdapter#healthy? returns true when connection and channel are open
Joist::Events::Adapters::AmazonMqAdapter#healthy? returns false when channel is closed
Joist::Events::Adapters::AmazonMqAdapter#healthy? returns false when connection is closed
Joist::Events::Adapters::Base custom adapter can implement publish
Joist::Events::Adapters::Base custom adapter can implement subscribe
Joist::Events::Publisher#dual_write? returns false when dual-write not configured
Joist::Events::Publisher#dual_write? returns true when dual-write configured
Joist::Events::Publisher dual-write mode publishes to all configured adapters
Joist::Events::Publisher dual-write mode raises error if any adapter fails
Joist::Events::Publisher dual-write mode returns true if all adapters succeed
Joist::Events::Publisher#publish passes attributes to Message creation
Joist::Events::Publisher#publish creates Message from hash payload
Joist::Events::Publisher.new uses default adapter if not specified
Joist::Events::Publisher.new uses specified adapter if provided
Joist::Events::Publisher.new creates a publisher for a topic
Joist::Events::Publisher.new when adapter is configured creates publisher with adapter
Joist::Events::Publisher#publish_batch returns count of successful publishes
Joist::Events::Serializers::JsonSerializer round-trip serialization maintains data integrity through serialize/deserialize cycle
Joist::Events::Middleware::Metrics#call re-raises errors after recording metrics
Joist::Events::Middleware::Metrics#call records failure metrics on error
Joist::Events::Middleware::Metrics#call includes message topic in tags
Joist::Events::Middleware::Metrics#call includes error class in failure tags
Joist::Events::Middleware::Metrics#call records timing even on failure
Joist::Events::Middleware::Metrics#call includes message id in tags
Joist::Events::Middleware::Metrics.new raises error if backend doesn't implement required methods
Joist::Events::Middleware::Base custom middleware can wrap block execution
Joist::Events::Middleware::Base custom middleware receives message and block
Joist::Events::Middleware::Base#call raises NotImplementedError if not overridden
Joist::Events::Middleware::Retry#call only retries specified error classes
Joist::Events::Middleware::Retry#call executes block successfully on first attempt
Joist::Events::Middleware::Retry#call raises error after max attempts exhausted
Joist::Events::Middleware::Retry.new raises error for invalid backoff_factor
Joist::Events::Middleware::Retry.new creates middleware with default options
Joist::Events::Middleware::Retry.new raises error for invalid base_delay
Joist::Events::Middleware::Retry.new raises error for invalid max_delay
Joist::Events::Middleware::Retry.new raises error for invalid max_attempts
